definition: Group samples by domain, batch, site, time bin, or environment, compute a distribution distance such as Maximum Mean Discrepancy over feature or embedding columns, then assign each train-eval pair the similarity between their domains. This audits support overlap at the domain level rather than only sample-neighbor similarity.
example_usage: python domain_distribution_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --domain-col site --feature-cols-prefix feat_

citations.title: Maximum Mean Discrepancy for Generalization in the Presence of Distribution and Missingness Shift

evidence.summary: The paper proposes using MMD between training and test sets to address covariate and missingness shift.
The method introduces MMD as the core distributional discrepancy.

citations.title: Interpretable Distribution Shift Detection using Optimal Transport

evidence.summary: The paper uses optimal transport to detect and characterize train-test distribution shifts.

limitations: MMD is domain-level; it does not identify which individual training sample explains an evaluation sample.
Feature scaling and kernel bandwidth strongly affect the distance.
Small domains produce noisy distribution estimates.
quality_gates: Report domain definition, feature columns, scaling, kernel, gamma, and sample counts per domain.
Run sensitivity analysis over kernel bandwidth or use median-distance heuristics.
Use this alongside a sample-level nearest-neighbor axis when both are meaningful.
